rulings.Case Title: MELVIN DESIN VS. ASBESTOS CORPORATION LIMITED et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For An Order Extending The Discovery Cutoff For The Limited Purpose Of Deposing A Key Witness On Shortened Notice And Conducting A Site Inspection On Shortened Notice
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Discovery Calendar for Wednesday, June 27, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:00 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Kelly Moore Paint Co.¿s motion to extend discovery for limited purposes: hearing required. The hearing will be at 10:30am, not 9:00am. Both sides must bring proposed orders to the hearing. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: MELVIN DESIN VS. ASBESTOS CORPORATION LIMITED et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Plaintiff'S Motion To Compel Fdcc California, Inc. To Produce Custodian(S) Of Records And Person(S) Most Knowledgeable For Deposition And Responsive Documents
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Discovery Calendar for Wednesday, June 27, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:00 a.m., Line 1. Plaintiff¿s motion to compel FDCC California, Inc. to produce custodian of records and person most knowledgeable for deposition and responsive documents is off calendar as moot given the court¿s ruling on defendant¿s motion for summary judgment. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To General Order 129 Standard Plaintiff Interrogatories To Defendants From Woodard Chevrolet, Inc.
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Discovery Calendar for Wednesday, June 27, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:00 a.m., Line 2. Plaintiff¿s motion to compel responses to General Order 129 standard plaintiff interrogatories to defendants from defendant Woodard Chevrolet, Inc. is granted. Defendant has 30 days from the date of this hearing to provide verified responses. General Order 129 requires that defendant provide such responses. Plaintiff¿s request that hearings on any dispositive motions filed by defendant in asbestos cases before this court be stayed until 60 days after defendant has provided verified responses is granted. Plaintiff¿s request for sanctions is denied. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 10:30am, not 9:00am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To General Order 129 Standard Plaintiff Interrogatories To Defendants From American Auto Supply, Inc.
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Discovery Calendar for Wednesday, June 27, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:00 a.m., Line 2. Plaintiff¿s motion to compel responses to General Order 129 standard plaintiff interrogatories to defendants from American Auto Supply, Inc., for an order staying defendant from having heard any dispositive motions in asbestos cases before this court until 60 days after defendant has provided verified responses, and for sanctions of $961 is granted. No opposition filed. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 10:30am, not 9:00am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
